accept: rollback in WriteOpsAndAutoCommit tracks a separate 'staged' slice; unstage runs only on entries that were successfully staged.
spurious 'git restore --staged' stderr no longer appears when a partial-stage failure forces rollback.
existing rollback tests still pass; ideally add a regression test that captures stderr to assert no noise.
payload.description: Reviewer finding from act-c26a: in cli.WriteOpsAndAutoCommit (internal/cli/util.go:225-231), the rollback closure calls unstage(gops, p) for every path in written[], including paths written but not yet staged when stage fails mid-loop. unstage runs 'git restore --staged <path>' which exits non-zero if the file was never staged; the error is discarded so atomicity is preserved, but a spurious git stderr line is emitted. The cleaner pattern is writeBlockOpsViaInterface in internal/mcp/composed.go (480-485), which tracks a separate staged[] slice and only unstages files that successfully passed StageOpFile. Apply the same pattern to WriteOpsAndAutoCommit. Affects act_block and the new --blocked-by path equally.
payload.title: WriteOpsAndAutoCommit rollback: unstage called on never-staged files
